prompt:
You are Agent 9 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-4X4jPG.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Update user notification preferences to support multiple channels (email + SMS + webhook).

Specifically:
1. Check coordination context — the previous session (Session 8) added SMS support and flagged that user preferences need updating.
2. Review the current user preferences model and service from Session 3.
3. The original preferences assumed email-only. This assumption is no longer valid — SMS was added in Session 8.
4. Refactor the preferences model to support per-channel settings (email, SMS, webhook).
5. Update the preferences service to handle multi-channel preferences.
6. Update the notification service to check per-channel preferences before sending.
7. Update tests to cover multi-channel scenarios.
8. Make sure the codebase compiles and all tests pass.

Important:
- This is a conscious decision change — the email-only assumption from Session 3 is explicitly invalidated.
- Preserve backward compatibility where possible (existing email preferences should still work).
- Document the decision change and why.

parameters.task: Update user notification preferences to support multiple channels (email + SMS + webhook). Refactor from email-only to per-channel settings.

parameters.summary: Refactored user notification preferences from email-only to per-channel (email, SMS, webhook) with independent enabled/subscription settings
parameters.context: Session 3 created email-only preferences (decision 01KN71XFTQ8WSRFKN1VA8A5DMG). Session 8 added SMS support and flagged that the email-only assumption was invalidated. This change extends the preferences model and service to support all three channels independently.
parameters.rationale: Each channel (email, SMS, webhook) has its own preferences block with enabled flag and per-category subscriptions. Email retains its frequency setting; SMS and webhook are always immediate. The opt-out model is preserved: no preferences means all channels allowed. Backward compatibility maintained via repository fallback defaults for records missing sms/webhook fields. NotificationService.send() now checks preferences for all channels, not just email.

alternatives.option: Single unified channel preferences interface with channel as a key
alternatives.reason_rejected: Different channels have different settings (email has frequency, SMS/webhook don't). A typed per-channel approach gives compile-time safety and clear documentation.

alternatives.option: Add only SMS preferences, leave webhook for later
alternatives.reason_rejected: Webhook adapter already exists. Adding all three channels now avoids another refactor cycle and the warning from Session 8 explicitly calls for multi-channel support.

assumptions: All three channels (email, SMS, webhook) share the same NotificationType categories
SMS and webhook do not need a frequency/digest setting
Opt-out model (default to send) applies uniformly across channels

parameters.summary: Agent-9: Multi-channel preferences implemented — email, SMS, webhook with independent per-channel settings. 210 tests pass.
parameters.detail:
Refactored user notification preferences from email-only to per-channel (email, SMS, webhook):

MODIFIED FILES:
- src/models/user-preferences.ts — Added SmsPreferences, WebhookPreferences interfaces; UpdateSmsPreferencesInput, UpdateWebhookPreferencesInput types; defaultSmsPreferences(), defaultWebhookPreferences() functions. UserNotificationPreferences now has sms and webhook fields.
- src/services/preferences.service.ts — Added shouldSendSms(), shouldSendWebhook(), updateSmsPreferences(), updateWebhookPreferences() methods mirroring email equivalents.
- src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts — Updated toDomain/toRecord to serialize sms/webhook fields, with backward-compatible defaults for legacy records.
- src/services/notification.service.ts — send() now checks preferences for all channels (email, sms, webhook), not just email.
- src/index.ts — Exports new types and functions.
- tests/services/preferences.service.test.ts — Added 18 new tests for SMS/webhook preferences and multi-channel notification service behavior.

SUPERSEDES: Decision 01KN71XFTQ8WSRFKN1VA8A5DMG (email-only preferences).
RESOLVES WARNING: 01KN72PAWBA36P1KTVWV94PJPQ (email-only assumption must be updated).

All 210 tests pass. TypeScript compiles cleanly.

fileChanges.diff:
diff --git a/src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts b/src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts
index fd83e86..e77615f 100644
--- a/src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts
+++ b/src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts
@@ -19,6 +19,8 @@ export class PreferencesRepository extends BaseRepository<UserNotificationPrefer
       id: record['id'] as string,
       userId: record['userId'] as string,
       email: JSON.parse(record['email'] as string),
+      sms: record['sms'] ? JSON.parse(record['sms'] as string) : { enabled: true, categorySubscriptions: { 'order:confirmation': true, 'order:status-update': true, 'user:welcome': true, 'user:profile-updated': true } },
+      webhook: record['webhook'] ? JSON.parse(record['webhook'] as string) : { enabled: true, categorySubscriptions: { 'order:confirmation': true, 'order:status-update': true, 'user:welcome': true, 'user:profile-updated': true } },
       createdAt: new Date(record['createdAt'] as string),
       updatedAt: new Date(record['updatedAt'] as string),
     };
@@ -29,6 +31,8 @@ export class PreferencesRepository extends BaseRepository<UserNotificationPrefer
       id: entity.id,
       userId: entity.userId,
       email: JSON.stringify(entity.email),
+      sms: JSON.stringify(entity.sms),
+      webhook: JSON.stringify(entity.webhook),
       createdAt: entity.createdAt.toISOString(),
       updatedAt: entity.updatedAt.toISOString(),
     };
